title: Execution visibility boundary
law_statement: A persistent transition is externally visible only after validation, authority or role checks, epoch assignment when needed, durable WAL coverage, and the publication boundary for that transition group.
runtime_boundary: dispatch/prepare -> WAL append/sync or durable cut -> canonical RAM mutation -> publish/replay/client emission
visibility_rule: Observers, clients, mesh peers, and replay tape must not see a transition that recovery from the durable cut cannot reproduce.

current_gates.proves: single update visibility waits for the durable watermark
remote handoff emission is deferred until same-batch writes are included
live mesh adoption is required for the cross-broker product path

live Agar path can join, command, cross seams, preserve real worker ownership, reject privilege self-assignment, acknowledge commands after handoff, render moving CLIENT-stream pixels, emit a replay_eval-clean live replay tape, restore-dryrun the demo WAL, and compare a restarted broker InspectorFrame with the final live broker/client cut

fail_under_broken.breakage: publish or mesh-send before durable coverage
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: observer or handoff frame sees state that recovery cannot reproduce

fail_under_broken.breakage: live game command, ownership visibility, replay breadcrumb, WAL recovery, restored broker agreement, or public pixels cross a seam without the current owner/client stream agreeing
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: gate reports command_after_handoff_ok=false, probe player disappeared before seam proof, unknown_owner_frames, duplicate_frames, failed security negative probe, replay_eval error, restore dry-run error, empty recovered store, unknown WAL kind, restored entity count/id/position/logical-owner mismatch, blank canvas, missing CLIENT-stream source, or unmoving visible player

known_gaps: not every persistent op is table-driven through one shared execution-law test yet
non_scope: does not require lock-free broker execution
does not define a binary protocol

title: Component authority consistency
law_statement: The writer for a component is unique at a given entity/component/epoch, entity ownership never replaces per-component authority, and authority groups move atomically only when explicitly defined.
runtime_boundary: authority map, worker cache, handoff, mesh adoption, client cache, and snapshot restore
visibility_rule: A stale owner write must be rejected, a new owner write must use the current epoch, and physics authority groups cannot split across owners during a handoff.

current_gates.proves: physics island components are fenced together
old owner writes remain rejected after recovery
dense seam ownership is conserved across matching workers

fail_under_broken.breakage: old and new owners both accept the same component epoch
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: stale write overwrites recovered authority state

fail_under_broken.breakage: physics authority migrates only pos/vel while rot/lin/ang/at_rest remain old-owned
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: the component epoch set is incomplete

known_gaps: authority group mapping is still partly string-derived and should become a typed contract
non_scope: does not make every component migrate together

title: Durable ordering beats wall clock
law_statement: Recovery and replay order are determined by epoch, WAL order, replay order, and activation tick; wall-clock time is telemetry and must not decide recovered truth.
runtime_boundary: WAL reader, restore offset, replay tape, snapshot marker, partition map activation
visibility_rule: A restore cut replays records before the cut, ignores records after the cut, and rejects ambiguous or future WAL versions.

current_gates.proves: snapshot marker cut restores marker-time state

fail_under_broken.breakage: restore uses wall clock or full file instead of the named WAL cut
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: post-cut entity appears in restored marker-time state

fail_under_broken.breakage: future WAL version is accepted as current truth
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: reader accepts an incompatible future log

known_gaps: activation tick is not yet a fully typed PartitionMap promotion contract
non_scope: does not promise deterministic physics integration across platforms

title: Failure cases fail closed or replay exactly
law_statement: Crash mid-op, WAL partial write, duplicate mesh delivery, partial mesh transfer, reconnect replay, and stale retry must either fail closed before publication or replay to exactly one recoverable state.
runtime_boundary: WAL append/sync, compaction, mesh_out/mesh_acked, pending handoffs, reconnect/client cache
visibility_rule: A failure may reject or retry, but it must not create double ownership, resurrect departed local state, or silently drop a durable pending transition.

current_gates.proves: mesh out WAL failure does not send or remove local entity
duplicate old upstream mesh resend does not re-adopt an onward-moved entity
compaction does not cut away pending durable transitions
durable delete tombstone dominates an earlier queued local handoff after WAL recovery

fail_under_broken.breakage: mesh send/removal happens after a failed mesh_out WAL write
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: local entity disappears or remote sees an unrecoverable handoff

fail_under_broken.breakage: compaction snapshots canonical RAM while pending durable transitions are not cut
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: recovery loses a staged or pending transition

fail_under_broken.breakage: queued handoff transfer is replayed after a later delete tombstone
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: recovery resurrects a deleted entity or creates pending mesh/fence state

known_gaps: full multi-broker crash/reconnect chaos matrix is not automated yet
non_scope: does not make every network retry exactly-once at transport level

title: Runtime artifacts stay redacted, versioned, and replayable
law_statement: Runtime state may become telemetry, replay tape, snapshot, or model-plane feature data only through versioned artifacts that name ownership/provenance, redact secrets and payload bodies when required, and stay replayable or explicitly diagnostic-only.
runtime_boundary: replay tape, SnapshotManifest, component registry, client bridge fixtures, model-plane inputs
visibility_rule: A downstream artifact must not contain auth tokens or raw component bodies when the contract says summary only, and model-plane data must not become a hidden control plane.

current_gates.proves: replay tape redacts tokens and component bodies
large payloads are summarized by size
snapshot manifest exposes typed versioned contract fields
model-plane actions are typed proposals and cannot name direct runtime mutations
model-plane proposals require provenance and guarded mode requires a validator

fail_under_broken.breakage: replay tape records auth_token or full component payload body
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: redaction assertion finds secret or body in the tape

fail_under_broken.breakage: snapshot manifest omits typed version/schema metadata
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: typed accessors cannot reconstruct the artifact contract

fail_under_broken.breakage: model-plane action accepts direct authority, component, mesh, partition activation, or WAL mutation
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: forbidden runtime mutation name parses as an allowed model action

fail_under_broken.breakage: guarded model proposal can be emitted without validator provenance
fail_under_broken.expected_failure: guarded proposal validates without a validator id

known_gaps: model-plane feature block schema is not implemented yet
model proposals are not yet wired to replay/eval or partition-map validators
non_scope: does not train or ship a model
does not make replay_eval a full reducer replay engine
does not let model-plane proposals mutate runtime state
